mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed 1-year moving average
failure_condition: Joint exceedance of 1-year moving averages fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 60% of historical cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate output and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or the signal reverses direction during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 320 and the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, the resulting stagflationary pressure forces aggressive portfolio de-risking, generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered observations, or the joint threshold condition shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic expansion and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to generate positive 60-day returns in > 65% of months, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 across multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth falls below GDPC1 annualized growth
failure_condition: The divergence metric fails to predict negative returns, or 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of divergence instances during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant when inflation expectations exceed 3.0%.
